title: 📐 K-12 Math Mastery - Complete Curriculum Template
content:
# 📐 K-12 Math Mastery - Complete Curriculum Template

K-12 Math Mastery Template
Complete mathematics curriculum tracker from Kindergarten through 12th grade
Perfect for:
Homeschool parents tracking student progress
Teachers organizing curriculum units
Students building a personal math portfolio
Tutors managing multiple students
🎯 How to Use This Template
For Parents/Teachers:
Navigate to your student's grade level section below
Check off mastered concepts as you teach them
Use the practice problems database to assign work
Track progress with the built-in assessment tracker
For Students:
Find your current grade level
Work through concepts in order (or jump to areas you need help with)
Complete practice problems and log your scores
Celebrate milestones as you master new skills
📊 Quick Grade Level Navigator
Elementary (K-5):
Kindergarten - Counting, shapes, patterns
Grade 1 - Addition, subtraction to 20
Grade 2 - Place value, 2-digit operations
Grade 3 - Multiplication, division, fractions intro
Grade 4 - Multi-digit multiplication, equivalent fractions
Grade 5 - Decimals, division with remainders, volume
Middle School (6-8):
Grade 6 - Ratios, negative numbers, expressions
Grade 7 - Proportions, percentages, linear equations
Grade 8 - Functions, Pythagorean theorem, exponents
High School (9-12):
Algebra I - Linear equations, quadratics, polynomials
Geometry - Proofs, triangles, circles, transformations
Algebra II - Advanced functions, matrices, logarithms
Pre-Calculus - Trigonometry, polar coordinates, sequences
Calculus - Limits, derivatives, integrals, series
Statistics - Data analysis, probability, distributions
🧮 Elementary Mathematics
Kindergarten {#kindergarten}
Counting & Number Sense
Count to 10
Count to 20
Count to 100 by 1s and 10s
Recognize written numbers 0-20
Write numbers 0-20
Understand one-to-one correspondence
Compare numbers using more/less/equal
Operations & Algebraic Thinking
Understand addition as putting together
Understand subtraction as taking apart
Decompose numbers less than 10 (5 = 3 + 2)
Make 10 in multiple ways
Solve simple word problems with objects
Geometry
Identify and describe shapes (circle, square, triangle, rectangle)
Distinguish between 2D and 3D shapes
Compare shapes using informal language (bigger, smaller)
Build and draw shapes
Measurement & Data
Compare objects by length, weight
Sort objects into categories
Count objects in each category
Create simple bar graphs
Grade 1 {#grade-1}
Operations & Algebraic Thinking
Master addition facts to 20
Master subtraction facts to 20
Understand the relationship between addition and subtraction
Solve word problems with three numbers (2 + 3 + 4)
Determine unknown numbers in equations (__ + 3 = 8)
Number & Operations in Base Ten
Count to 120
Understand place value (tens and ones)
Add two-digit and one-digit numbers
Mentally find 10 more or 10 less
Compare two-digit numbers using >, <, =
Measurement & Data
Order three objects by length
Measure lengths using non-standard units
Tell time to the hour and half-hour
Organize and interpret data with up to three categories
Geometry
Distinguish defining attributes of shapes
Compose shapes to create composite shapes
Partition circles and rectangles into halves and quarters
Grade 2 {#grade-2}
Operations & Algebraic Thinking
Fluently add and subtract within 20
Solve one-step word problems within 100
Solve two-step word problems within 100
Determine odd/even numbers
Use arrays to understand repeated addition
Number & Operations in Base Ten
Understand place value to 1000 (hundreds, tens, ones)
Skip-count by 5s, 10s, and 100s
Read and write numbers to 1000
Compare three-digit numbers
Add and subtract within 1000
Mentally add/subtract 10 or 100
Measurement & Data
Measure using standard units (inches, centimeters)
Estimate lengths
Tell time to nearest 5 minutes
Count money (dollar bills, quarters, dimes, nickels, pennies)
Create line plots
Solve word problems involving length and money
Geometry
Recognize triangles, quadrilaterals, pentagons, hexagons
Draw shapes with specific attributes
Partition rectangles into equal squares
Partition circles and rectangles into halves, thirds, fourths
Grade 3 {#grade-3}
Operations & Algebraic Thinking
Understand multiplication as repeated addition
Understand division as partitioning
Fluently multiply and divide within 100
Solve two-step word problems using all four operations
Identify arithmetic patterns
Number & Operations in Base Ten
Round whole numbers to nearest 10 or 100
Fluently add and subtract within 1000
Multiply one-digit by multiples of 10 (3 × 70)
Number & Operations - Fractions
Understand fractions as parts of a whole
Represent fractions on a number line
Understand equivalent fractions (1/2 = 2/4)
Compare fractions with same numerator or denominator
Measurement & Data
Tell time to the nearest minute
Solve word problems involving time intervals
Measure and estimate liquid volumes and masses
Create scaled picture graphs and bar graphs
Measure areas by counting unit squares
Understand perimeter as boundary measurement
Geometry
Understand shapes have shared attributes
Classify quadrilaterals by their properties
Partition shapes into equal areas
Grade 4 {#grade-4}
Operations & Algebraic Thinking
Use multiplication to solve word problems
Find factor pairs for numbers 1-100
Identify prime and composite numbers
Generate and analyze number patterns
Number & Operations in Base Ten
Understand place value to 1,000,000
Compare multi-digit numbers
Round multi-digit numbers
Fluently add and subtract multi-digit numbers
Multiply up to 4-digit by 1-digit numbers
Multiply two 2-digit numbers
Divide up to 4-digit by 1-digit numbers
Number & Operations - Fractions
Explain equivalent fractions
Compare fractions with different numerators and denominators
Add and subtract fractions with like denominators
Multiply fractions by whole numbers
Understand decimal notation (tenths, hundredths)
Compare decimal numbers
Measurement & Data
Convert measurements within one system
Solve word problems involving measurement
Apply area and perimeter formulas for rectangles
Create line plots with fractional measurements
Understand angle measurement
Measure and sketch angles
Geometry
Draw points, lines, line segments, rays, angles
Identify perpendicular and parallel lines
Classify shapes by properties of their lines and angles
Identify lines of symmetry
Grade 5 {#grade-5}
Operations & Algebraic Thinking
Write and interpret numerical expressions with parentheses
Analyze patterns and relationships
Create coordinate graphs from rules
Number & Operations in Base Ten
Understand place value to 1,000,000,000
Use exponents to denote powers of 10
Read, write, and compare decimals to thousandths
Round decimals
Fluently multiply multi-digit whole numbers
Divide multi-digit numbers with remainders
Add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals to hundredths
Number & Operations - Fractions
Add and subtract fractions with unlike denominators
Solve word problems with fraction addition/subtraction
Multiply fractions by fractions
Multiply fractions by whole numbers
Divide unit fractions by whole numbers
Divide whole numbers by unit fractions
Measurement & Data
Convert measurements across different units
Create line plots with fractional measurements
Understand concepts of volume
Measure volumes using unit cubes
Apply formulas V = l × w × h and V = B × h
Geometry
Graph points on the coordinate plane
Classify 2D figures by properties
Understand hierarchy of shape categories
🔢 Middle School Mathematics
Grade 6 {#grade-6}
Ratios & Proportional Relationships
Understand ratio concepts
Understand unit rate
Use ratio tables to solve problems
Find percentages of quantities
Convert between fractions, decimals, and percentages
The Number System
Divide fractions by fractions
Fluently divide multi-digit numbers
Add, subtract, multiply, divide multi-digit decimals
Find greatest common factor (GCF)
Find least common multiple (LCM)
Understand positive and negative numbers
Plot integers on a number line
Find absolute value
Graph points in all four quadrants
Expressions & Equations
Write and evaluate numerical expressions with exponents
Write expressions using variables
Identify equivalent expressions
Solve equations of the form x + p = q and px = q
Write inequalities
Represent relationships with equations
Geometry
Find area of triangles
Find area of special quadrilaterals
Find area of polygons by composing/decomposing
Find volume of rectangular prisms with fractional edges
Draw polygons in the coordinate plane
Find surface area using nets
Statistics & Probability
Recognize statistical questions
Understand measures of center (mean, median, mode)
Display numerical data in plots on a number line
Understand measures of variation (range, interquartile range)
Grade 7 {#grade-7}
Ratios & Proportional Relationships
Compute unit rates with complex fractions
Recognize and represent proportional relationships
Identify proportional relationships in tables, graphs, equations
Identify constant of proportionality
Solve multi-step ratio and percent problems
Calculate simple interest
Calculate tax, tips, markups, markdowns
The Number System
Add and subtract integers
Multiply and divide integers
Apply properties of operations with integers
Convert rational numbers to decimals
Add, subtract, multiply, divide rational numbers
Solve real-world problems with rational numbers
Expressions & Equations
Apply properties to simplify linear expressions
Solve equations of the form px + q = r and p(x + q) = r
Solve word problems leading to equations
Solve inequalities of the form px + q > r
Graph solutions to inequalities on a number line
Geometry
Draw geometric shapes with given conditions
Describe 2D cross-sections of 3D objects
Solve problems involving scale drawings
Draw triangles from given conditions
Calculate area and circumference of circles
Use facts about angles (complementary, supplementary, vertical)
Solve problems with angles in triangles
Find surface area of 3D objects
Calculate volume of prisms and pyramids
Statistics & Probability
Use random sampling to make inferences about populations
Compare two populations using measures of center and variation
Understand probability of chance events
Approximate probability using collected data
Develop probability models
Find probabilities of compound events
Grade 8 {#grade-8}
The Number System
Understand irrational numbers
Approximate irrational numbers
Compare and order rational and irrational numbers
Expressions & Equations
Apply properties of integer exponents
Use square root and cube root symbols
Express numbers in scientific notation
Perform operations with scientific notation
Graph proportional relationships
Compare proportional relationships in different forms
Solve linear equations with one variable
Solve linear equations with rational coefficients
Analyze and solve pairs of simultaneous linear equations
Solve systems of equations by graphing
Solve systems of equations algebraically
Functions
Understand functions as rules
Compare functions in different forms
Interpret rate of change and initial value from tables, graphs, equations
Sketch graphs from verbal descriptions
Understand y = mx + b as defining a linear function
Geometry
Verify experimentally: properties of rotations, reflections, translations
Understand congruent figures
Understand similar figures
Use informal arguments to establish angle relationships
Explain a proof of the Pythagorean Theorem
Apply the Pythagorean Theorem
Find distance between two points on coordinate plane
Know formulas for volumes of cones, cylinders, spheres
Statistics & Probability
Construct and interpret scatter plots
Understand patterns in scatter plots (clustering, outliers, linear association)
Use linear models to solve problems
Understand patterns in two-way frequency tables
🎓 High School Mathematics
Algebra I {#algebra-i}
Number & Quantity
Use properties of rational and irrational numbers
Reason quantitatively and use units
Choose appropriate level of accuracy
Define and interpret variables
Algebra - Seeing Structure in Expressions
Interpret parts of expressions in context
Write expressions in equivalent forms
Factor and expand polynomials
Complete the square for quadratic expressions
Algebra - Arithmetic with Polynomials & Rational Expressions
Perform arithmetic operations on polynomials
Understand remainder theorem
Use polynomial identities
Rewrite rational expressions
Algebra - Creating Equations
Create equations and inequalities in one variable
Create equations in two or more variables
Represent constraints with equations or inequalities
Rearrange formulas to highlight a quantity of interest
Algebra - Reasoning with Equations & Inequalities
Solve linear equations and inequalities
Solve systems of linear equations
Solve quadratic equations
Graph and solve systems of linear inequalities
Functions - Interpreting Functions
Understand function notation
Interpret functions in context
Analyze graphs of functions
Calculate and interpret average rate of change
Functions - Building Functions
Build functions from context
Build new functions from existing functions
Identify effect of transformations on graphs
Functions - Linear, Quadratic & Exponential Models
Construct and compare linear and exponential models
Interpret expressions for functions in context
Distinguish between linear and exponential growth
Use logarithms to solve exponential equations
Statistics & Probability
Summarize, represent, and interpret categorical and quantitative data
Interpret linear models
Distinguish between correlation and causation
Geometry {#geometry}
Congruence
Experiment with transformations
Understand congruence in terms of rigid motions
Prove geometric theorems
Make geometric constructions
Similarity, Right Triangles & Trigonometry
Understand similarity in terms of transformations
Prove theorems involving similarity
Define trigonometric ratios
Apply trigonometry to general triangles
Circles
Understand and apply circle theorems
Find arc lengths and areas of sectors
Derive equation of a circle
Prove properties of tangent lines
Expressing Geometric Properties with Equations
Translate between geometric and algebraic descriptions
Use coordinates to prove geometric theorems
Derive equations of parabolas, ellipses, hyperbolas
Geometric Measurement & Dimension
Explain volume formulas and use them
Visualize relationships between 2D and 3D objects
Apply geometric concepts in modeling situations
Modeling with Geometry
Apply geometric methods to solve design problems
Use geometric shapes to model objects
Apply concepts of density
Algebra II {#algebra-ii}
Polynomials & Rational Functions
Perform operations on polynomials
Understand relationship between zeros and factors
Solve polynomial equations
Graph polynomial functions
Understand rational functions
Rewrite rational expressions
Exponential & Logarithmic Functions
Extend properties of exponents to rational exponents
Use properties of logarithms
Solve exponential and logarithmic equations
Model with exponential and logarithmic functions
Trigonometric Functions
Extend domain of trigonometric functions
Model periodic phenomena
Prove and apply trigonometric identities
Matrices
Perform operations on matrices
Understand matrix multiplication
Use matrices to represent and solve systems
Calculate determinants and inverses
Complex Numbers
Perform arithmetic operations with complex numbers
Represent complex numbers geometrically
Use complex numbers in polynomial identities
Solve quadratic equations with complex solutions
Conic Sections
Write equations of conic sections
Graph conic sections
Identify conic sections from equations
Solve systems involving conic sections
Sequences & Series
Understand arithmetic and geometric sequences
Find sums of finite and infinite series
Use sigma notation
Apply binomial theorem
Pre-Calculus {#pre-calculus}
Advanced Functions
Analyze piecewise functions
Understand composition of functions
Find inverse functions
Work with parametric equations
Trigonometry
Understand unit circle
Solve trigonometric equations
Prove trigonometric identities
Use sum and difference formulas
Apply double-angle and half-angle formulas
Graph trigonometric functions with transformations
Polar Coordinates
Convert between rectangular and polar coordinates
Graph polar equations
Understand polar form of complex numbers
Apply De Moivre's Theorem
Vectors
Represent vectors geometrically and algebraically
Perform vector operations
Calculate dot product and cross product
Use vectors to solve problems in physics
Limits & Continuity (Introduction)
Understand concept of limit
Evaluate limits algebraically
Understand continuity
Evaluate limits at infinity
Calculus {#calculus}
Limits & Continuity
Understand formal definition of limit
Evaluate limits using various techniques
Understand Intermediate Value Theorem
Identify discontinuities and their types
Derivatives
Understand definition of derivative
Calculate derivatives using limit definition
Apply power rule, product rule, quotient rule, chain rule
Find derivatives of trigonometric functions
Find derivatives of exponential and logarithmic functions
Find derivatives of inverse functions
Calculate implicit derivatives
Find derivatives of parametric and polar functions
Applications of Derivatives
Interpret meaning of derivative in context
Use derivatives to analyze graphs
Solve optimization problems
Solve related rates problems
Apply Mean Value Theorem
Use L'Hôpital's Rule
Integrals
Understand Riemann sums
Evaluate definite integrals
Understand Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
Find antiderivatives
Use substitution method
Use integration by parts
Evaluate improper integrals
Applications of Integrals
Calculate areas between curves
Find volumes using disk/washer method
Find volumes using shell method
Calculate arc length
Solve differential equations
Model with differential equations
Sequences & Series
Test series for convergence
Understand power series
Find Taylor and Maclaurin series
Apply series to approximate functions
Statistics {#statistics}
Exploring Data
Create and interpret graphs (histograms, box plots, scatter plots)
Calculate summary statistics
Understand measures of center and spread
Identify outliers
Describe distributions
Modeling Distributions
Understand normal distribution
Use z-scores
Apply empirical rule (68-95-99.7)
Assess normality
Describing Relationships
Calculate and interpret correlation coefficient
Find least-squares regression line
Interpret slope and y-intercept
Calculate and interpret residuals
Understand limitations of regression
Designing Studies
Understand sampling methods
Identify bias in sampling
Understand experimental design
Distinguish between observational studies and experiments
Probability
Calculate basic probabilities
Use addition rule and multiplication rule
Understand conditional probability
Use tree diagrams and Venn diagrams
Apply Bayes' Theorem
Random Variables
Calculate expected value
Calculate variance and standard deviation
Understand binomial distribution
Understand geometric distribution
Sampling Distributions
Understand sampling distribution of sample mean
Apply Central Limit Theorem
Understand sampling distribution of sample proportion
Inference
Calculate confidence intervals for means
Calculate confidence intervals for proportions
Conduct hypothesis tests for means
Conduct hypothesis tests for proportions
Understand Type I and Type II errors
Conduct chi-square tests
Conduct t-tests and ANOVA
🎯 Practice Problems Database
Use the database below to log practice problems, track completion, and monitor progress.
Suggested properties:
Problem Number
Grade Level (select)
Topic (multi-select)
Difficulty (Easy/Medium/Hard)
Date Assigned
Date Completed
Score/Grade
Notes
Needs Review (checkbox)
📈 Progress Tracking
Monthly Assessment Tracker
Create monthly checkpoints to assess mastery of concepts
Suggested properties:
Month
Grade Level
Topics Covered
Assessment Type (Quiz/Test/Project)
Score
Areas of Strength
Areas for Improvement
Parent/Teacher Notes
📚 Resources & Learning Tools
Recommended Online Resources
Free Resources:
Khan Academy - Comprehensive video lessons for all grades
IXL - Interactive practice problems (limited free access)
Math Playground - Games and logic puzzles
Desmos - Graphing calculator and activities
GeoGebra - Interactive geometry and algebra
Paid Resources:
Beast Academy - Elementary problem-solving curriculum
Art of Problem Solving - Advanced math for gifted students
Mathspace - Adaptive learning platform
Brilliant.org - Interactive STEM courses
Manipulatives & Tools
Physical Tools:
Base-10 blocks (place value)
Fraction bars and circles
Geometric solids
Protractor and compass
Graphing calculator (high school)
Digital Tools:
Virtual manipulatives (NCTM Illuminations)
Graphing calculator apps
Geometry construction software
Spreadsheet software for data analysis
💡 Teaching Tips & Strategies
For Parents/Teachers
Making Math Engaging:
Connect math to real-world situations (cooking, shopping, building)
Use games to practice facts and skills
Celebrate mistakes as learning opportunities
Show multiple solution methods
Let students explain their thinking
When Students Struggle:
Go back to concrete manipulatives
Break problems into smaller steps
Use visual models (number lines, area models, bar diagrams)
Practice mental math strategies
Revisit prerequisite skills
Building Number Sense:
Estimate before calculating
Look for patterns
Discuss reasonableness of answers
Compare multiple strategies
Build fluency through regular practice
Growth Mindset for Math
Key Messages:
"Everyone can learn math with effort and good strategies"
"Mistakes help your brain grow"
"Different people think about math differently - share your approach"
"Speed isn't the goal - understanding is"
"Ask questions when you're confused"
🎓 College & Career Readiness
Math Pathways After High School
STEM Careers (Requires strong math):
Engineering (all types)
Computer Science
Physics
Chemistry
Data Science
Actuarial Science
Economics
Careers Using Math Daily:
Architecture
Finance/Banking
Healthcare (nursing, pharmacy)
Skilled trades (electrician, plumber, carpenter)
Business/Management
Education
Standardized Test Prep
Tests to Consider:
SAT Math (Grades 11-12)
ACT Math (Grades 11-12)
AP Calculus AB/BC (Grade 12)
AP Statistics (Grades 11-12)
Prep Strategies:
Take practice tests to identify weak areas
Review concepts from earlier grades
Learn test-taking strategies
Practice time management
Use official practice materials
🏆 Celebrating Milestones
Elementary Achievements:
✅ Master addition/subtraction facts
✅ Master multiplication tables
✅ Understand fractions
✅ Solve multi-step word problems
Middle School Achievements:
✅ Fluent with all operations on fractions and decimals
✅ Solve equations and inequalities
✅ Understand and graph functions
✅ Apply Pythagorean theorem
High School Achievements:
✅ Complete Algebra I
✅ Complete Geometry with proofs
✅ Complete Algebra II
✅ Master trigonometry
✅ Complete Calculus
✅ Complete Statistics
Remember: Math is a journey, not a race. Focus on deep understanding over speed, celebrate progress, and foster a love of problem-solving!
Template created by: Issac Davis
For: Homeschool families, teachers, students, and lifelong learners
